paper_title: Influence of internal curing on compressive strength and drying shrinkage of supersulfated cement mortar

entities.description: Researcher at Can Tho University, Viet Nam.
Correspondence author at Can Tho University, Viet Nam.
Researcher at Can Tho University, Viet Nam.

entities.id: College of Engineering, Can Tho University
entities.description: Educational institution located in Viet Nam.
The study focuses on cementitious materials and their structural properties.

entities.id: Cold-bonded fly ash based artificial lightweight aggregate (ALWA)
entities.description: An internal curing agent produced from fly ash and OPC used to replace fine aggregate.
A binder produced with a mixture of slag, gypsum, and blended Portland cement.
A process of maintaining moisture within concrete/mortar using water-saturated porous materials.
A three-period manufacturing process (feeding, nucleation, maintenance) to create ALWA.
The ability of the cement mortar to withstand loads without failure.
The contraction of a hardened concrete mixture due to the loss of capillary water.
A phenomenon where water is consumed by hydration, leading to increased internal stress.
Average reduction in unit weight of IC-SSC mortars compared to reference.
Average reduction in dried density of hardened mortars compared to reference.
Relative compressive strength achieved at 50 vol.% ALWA replacement.
Specific water absorption capacity of the manufactured ALWA.
